flashcards.front: What are the three main stream classes for file handling in C++?
flashcards.back: ifstream (input file stream), ofstream (output file stream), and fstream (file stream for both input/output).

flashcards.front: What header file is required for file operations in C++?
flashcards.back: #include <fstream> header file provides file stream classes for file handling.

flashcards.front: How do you open a file using ofstream?
flashcards.back: ofstream outfile("filename.txt"); or outfile.open("filename.txt"); Opens file for writing only.

flashcards.front: How do you open a file using ifstream?
flashcards.back: ifstream infile("filename.txt"); or infile.open("filename.txt"); Opens file for reading only.

flashcards.front: What is the syntax to check if a file was opened successfully?
flashcards.back: Use fail() method: if(!outfile) or if(outfile.fail()) or simply: if(outfile.is_open()) returns true if file is open.

flashcards.front: How do you close a file in C++?
flashcards.back: Use the close() method: outfile.close(); or infile.close(); or fstreamfile.close();

flashcards.front: What are the file open modes in C++?
flashcards.back: ios::in (read), ios::out (write), ios::app (append), ios::trunc (truncate), ios::binary (binary mode), ios::ate (at end).
Append mode - data is added at the end of the file, preserving existing content.
Truncate mode - if file exists, its contents are discarded and length set to zero.

flashcards.front: What is the default file open mode for ofstream?
flashcards.back: ios::out | ios::trunc - opens file for writing and truncates existing content.

flashcards.front: What is the default file open mode for ifstream?
flashcards.back: ios::in - opens file for reading only.

flashcards.front: What is fstream class used for?
flashcards.back: fstream provides both input and output operations on a file. Requires explicit ios::in and ios::out modes.

flashcards.front: Why is it important to close files after use?
flashcards.back: Closes file buffer, flushes unwritten data, releases system resources, prevents data loss.

flashcards.front: What is the eof() function used for?
flashcards.back: eof() returns true when end-of-file is reached while reading from a file.

flashcards.front: What is binary mode file opening?
flashcards.back: Use ios::binary mode - opens file in binary mode instead of text mode, no newline conversions.
